name: Plate Pinch Hold
resistance_profile: Pinching smooth weight plates between the thumb and fingers loads the thumb adductors and forearm flexors isometrically, training pinch grip that curls miss.
cues: Squeeze through the whole thumb and fingertips
Keep the wrist neutral and the plates vertical
Hold until just before the grip gives out
execution_steps: Stand two smooth-sided weight plates together with the smooth faces pointing out.
Pinch them between your thumb on one side and fingers on the other, keeping the wrist neutral.
Lift the plates off the rack or floor and hold them at your side for time.
Set them down under control just before the grip fails, then switch hands or rest.
good_when: You want to train pinch grip and thumb strength that crush-grip work neglects
You want a simple, loadable grip finisher using only plates
bad_when: Only rubber-coated or handled plates are available, making the pinch too easy
You have thumb or wrist joint pain
common_errors: Resting the plates against the leg to cheat the hold
Letting the plates tilt so they slip apart
progressions: Add a plate or use heavier plates
